notes:
STANDARD XY-era set (xy11 / STS), shipEv=true. PACK STRUCTURE: 10 cards/pack confirmed via multiple retailer listings (Pokemon Center "Sleeved Booster (10 Cards)", eBay/Amazon "10 Cards/Pack"). XY structure = 1 rare-or-better, 3 uncommon, ~5 common, 1 reverse-holo (+1 basic Energy, unmodeled) = 10 value slots. Web search corroborated: "guaranteed at least 1 Rare and 3 Uncommon".

PRODUCTS: 36-pack booster box (standard, confirmed via eBay/Amazon "Box of 36 Packs") -> displayPacks=36. ETB confirmed via web search of multiple retailers + Amazon listing: "contains 8 Pokemon TCG: XY-Steam Siege booster packs" -> etbPacks=8.

COARSE-BUCKET MAPPING - EXACT match to engine's available buckets, verified against pokemontcg.io xy11 (RE-FETCHED via curl, 116 cards): Common 31, Uncommon 30, Rare 23, Rare Holo 9, Rare Ultra 8, Rare BREAK 7, Rare Holo EX 6, Rare Secret 2. Mapping: common(31), uncommon(30), rare(23), rare-holo(9), secret-rare(2). ultra-rare bucket(21) = Rare Ultra(8 full-arts) + Rare BREAK(7) + Rare Holo EX(6 EX/M-EX) = 21. EX, M-EX, full-art and BREAK all collapse to ultra-rare per the engine's lumping rule; this is an EXACT (21=21) match to the given bucket counts.

RARE-OR-BETTER SLOT (EV driver) - anchored on the SET-SPECIFIC box study at pokemoncards.com.au (n=24 booster boxes + 48 blisters + 1 ETB; RE-FETCHABLE, snippet verified verbatim via web search): per box "at least 2 to 3 BREAK cards, 2 to 3 EX cards, two full art cards, 7 to 8 holo cards, and a secret rare in every one out of three boxes". Converting per-box -> per-pack (/36):
- BREAK 2.5/box = 0.0694; EX 2.5/box = 0.0694; full-art 2/box = 0.0556. ultra-rare = 0.0694+0.0694+0.0556 = 0.1944 -> 0.194.
- secret-rare = 1 per 3 boxes = 1/108 = 0.00926 -> 0.009.
- rare-holo = 7.5 holo/box / 36 = 0.2083 -> 0.208.
- rare = remainder = 1 - 0.194 - 0.009 - 0.208 = 0.589. Slot sums to EXACTLY 1.0.
Per-box sanity: rare 0.589*36=21.2, rare-holo 0.208*36=7.5 (matches "7-8 holo"), ultra 0.194*36=7.0 (matches 2.5 BREAK+2.5 EX+2 FA), secret 0.009*36=0.32 (matches "1 in 3 boxes"). Internally consistent.

CROSS-CHECK vs Flipside Gaming XY BREAK-era review (RE-FETCHED verbatim): "By Ancient Origins, pull rates had dropped to approximately 1:6 packs for EXs ... approximately 1:12 packs for URs ... Break Pokemon ~1:12 booster packs ... Secret Rares retained 1:72 pack rate". Flipside's 1:6 EX (=0.167) is the Ancient Origins PEAK EX density; Steam Siege has only 6 EX printings (vs more in AO), so the set-specific study's lower EX/ultra rate (0.194 combined ultra) is the better anchor and sits between Flipside's 1:12 BREAK+1:12 UR (~0.166) floor and a 1:6-EX-driven ceiling (~0.33). Flipside secret 1:72=0.0139 is close to and brackets the set study's 0.009; kept the set-specific 0.009.

FILLER SLOTS: 5 common (common=1.0), 3 uncommon (uncommon=1.0), 1 reverse-holo modeled 50/50 common/uncommon filler (sums to 1.0; engine convention, matches sun-moon/shining-legends configs); 10th card = basic Energy, not modeled.

CONFIDENCE: medium - set-specific n=24-box study (real, re-fetchable, snippet-verified) independently bracketed by Flipside's XY BREAK-era per-pack rates. Honest cap below 'high' because the 24-box study is a community sample reported as ranges (2-3 EX/BREAK), not a large per-pack-logged study with CIs.
